PrimerCorrectorPowderBlushBronzer

Let’s talk make-up - Face

PrimerCorrectorPowderBlushBronzer
Module 2

Let’s talk make-up - Face

Lesson 1 - The Primer

OBC Primer

What it looks like:

Product Overview

The ultimate blend of skin care and a perfecting primer.

A fresh and light formula containing Niacinamide and Hyaluronic acid, that help to brighten, firm and plump the skin, while functioning as a primer to provide adhesion for make-up – all with a silky finish.

Skincare benefits over time include a strengthened skin barrier, more elastic, firm and plump skin, as well as improved skin hydration.

What it does

  • Prepares the skin for make-up.
  • Helps make-up last longer by helping it grip to the skin.
  • Brightens, firms, and plumps the skin over time.
  • Leaves the skin feeling smooth with a silky finish.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Want their make-up to last longer.
  • Want smoother, fresher-looking skin.
  • Who like skincare benefits in their make-up routine.
  • Want a light, non-heavy feel on the skin.

How to sell

  • “This helps your make-up last longer and keeps your skin looking smooth.”
  • “It works like skincare and a primer in one step.”
  • “It’s great if you want a fresh, silky base before applying make-up.”

Top Tip

“It contains ingredients that help hydrate and brighten the skin, so your skin looks better even without make-up.”

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario (4 decisions): “My make-up doesn’t last… and I don’t want anything heavy”

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
“My make-up doesn’t last the whole day. But I don’t want anything that feels heavy on my skin.”

Your goal: Recommend the right product and explain it clearly.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the customer’s main need. What is the customer mainly asking for?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best matches this need?

3. Decision 3: Explain what it does (plain language). The customer asks: “What will it do for me?” What’s the best answer?

4. Decision 4: Close the recommendation. Choose the best closing line.

Choose an answer, then check the question.

Lesson 2 - The Flawless Corrector

OBC the flawless corrector

What it looks like:

Product Overview

A creamy, lightweight emulsion based on two innovative technologies: A mix of oils that instantly melt into the skin, for an immediate skincare fix; and treated pigments, resulting in the perfect corrective concealer.

Available in two shades - orange and pink - that effectively correct dark circles and dyschromia

  • Orange to correct grey-green dyschromia.
  • Pink to correct blue-violet dyschromia.

Is delicate on the skin, with a soft and velvety finish for a second-skin effect.

What it does

  • Corrects dark circles and uneven skin tone.
  • Melts into the skin for a natural, second-skin finish.
  • Helps neutralise unwanted colour under the eyes or on the face.
  • Feels light and comfortable on the skin with a soft, velvety finish.

Who is it for

Customers:

  • With dark circles under the eyes.
  • With uneven skin tone or discolouration (dyschromia).
  • Who want natural-looking correction, not heavy coverage.
  • Who want a lightweight, comfortable feel on the skin.

Shade guidance

  • Orange: Corrects grey-green tones.
  • Pink: Corrects blue-violet tones (common under the eyes).

How to sell

  • “This helps cancel out dark circles before you apply concealer.”
  • “It melts into the skin, so it looks natural, not heavy.”
  • “You use this first to correct the colour, then apply concealer on top.”

Top Tip

If the darkness looks more blue or purple, choose pink. If it looks dull or grey, choose orange.

When to recommend

Recommend this when a customer says concealer alone doesn’t fully cover their dark circles.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario (4 decisions): “My concealer still doesn’t cover my dark circles”

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
“I’ve tried concealer, but my dark circles still show through. I want it to look natural, not heavy.”

Your goal: Pick the right corrector shade, explain what it does, and guide how to use it.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the real issue: What is the customer struggling with?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best solves this problem?

3. Decision 3: Pick the correct shade. You look at the darkness and it appears more blue‑violet. Which shade do you recommend?

4. Decision 4: Explain how to use it (simple steps) The customer asks: “Do I use this instead of concealer?” What’s the best response?

Choose an answer, then check the question.

Lesson 3 - Loose powder

OBC loose powder

What it looks like:

Product Overview

A loose powder coming in two universal shades – banana and translucent - that match any complexion.

Has a smooth, silky texture while also containing spherical powders for an even application,

The translucent version is ideal to set foundation, making it last longer and giving the complexion an even appearance.

The Banana version is the perfect product to finish a more sculpted and shaped look, as well as acting as a colour corrector for grey or dull areas of the face, especially around the eyes.

The Loose Powder helps to absorb and regulate excess sebum, with a selection of raw materials to promote the physiological hydration of the skin.

Can be applied with a powder puff for a more even finish or with a powder brush for a natural look and quick touch-up.

What is does

  • Sets make-up and helps it last longer.
  • Gives the skin an even, smooth appearance.
  • Absorbs and controls excess oil (sebum).
  • Helps keep the skin hydrated while reducing shine.
  • Applies evenly with a soft, silky finish.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Want their foundation to last longer.
  • Get oily or shiny during the day.
  • Want a smooth, finished look.
  • Want either a natural finish or a more sculpted look.

Shade Guidance

Translucent:

  • Sets foundation.
  • Gives an even, natural finish.
  • Works for all skin tones.

Banana:

  • Finishes a more sculpted, shaped look.
  • Helps brighten and correct dull or grey areas.
  • Especially good around the eyes.

How to sell

  • “This sets your make-up and helps it last longer.”
  • “It keeps shine under control without drying your skin.”
  • “You can wear it natural or more sculpted, depending on how you apply it.”

Top Tip

Use banana to brighten and sculpt. Use translucent to set and smooth.

When to recommend

Recommend this when a customer says their make-up gets shiny, moves, or doesn’t last the whole day.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: “My make-up gets shiny by lunchtime”

Set the scene

You’re on the shop floor when a customer says:

C
“My foundation looks fine in the morning, but by midday I’m shiny and my make-up moves.”

Your goal: Recommend the right powder, explain the benefit clearly, and help the customer choose the correct shade and application.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the main problem. What is the customer’s key concern?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best solves this problem?

3. Decision 3: Select the right shade. The customer adds: “I want my skin to look natural, not heavy.” Which shade should you recommend?

4. Decision 4: Explain how to use it. The customer asks: “How should I apply it?” What is the best response?

Choose an answer, then check the question.

Lesson 4 - Blush duo 2

OBC blush duo 2

What it looks like:

Product Overview

Blush takes on a new visual and sensory experience in the form of two complementary formulas, each with unique finish and performance.

The Blush Duo also takes care of the skin by maintaining the physiological hydration and adding a delicate barrier effect.

Cream Blush

  • A creamy, smoothing and luminous formula with amino acid derivatives for a soft, silky finish and perfect adhesion to skin.
  • Contains ceramides with moisturizing and anti-ageing properties as well as Hyaluronic acid.
  • A light, sensorial and comfortable texture with a luminous, natural glow finish.

Compact Powder Blush

  • A light, impalpable and velvety compact powder with a matte, soft and silky finish.
  • Contains a mix of emollient oils and waxes, making it easy to blend.

What it does

  • Gives a healthy flush of colour to the cheeks.
  • Offers two finishes in one product: luminous cream and soft matte powder.
  • Helps keep the skin hydrated and comfortable.
  • Leaves the skin looking smooth, fresh, and naturally glowing.

What’s it for

Customers who:

  • Want a natural, healthy glow.
  • Like flexibility (cream and powder in one product).
  • Want blush that feels comfortable on the skin.
  • Want either a fresh glow or a more defined finish.

How to sell

Cream Blush

  • Gives a luminous, natural glow.
  • Melts into the skin for a smooth, silky finish.
  • Feels light and comfortable on the skin.

Sell it as: “Perfect for a fresh, glowing look.”

Compact Powder Blush

  • Gives a soft, matte finish.
  • Easy to blend for a smooth result.
  • Helps add definition and polish.

Sell it as “Great to finish the look or add extra definition.”

When to Recommend

Recommend this when a customer wants a healthy glow with the option to build or set their look.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: “I want a healthy glow, not too much make-up”

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
“I want my skin to look fresh and healthy. I don’t want anything heavy, but I still want some colour.”

Your goal: Recommend the right blush option and explain how to use it.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the customer’s goal. What is the customer mainly looking for?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best meets this need?

3. Decision 3: Select the best formula to start with. The customer adds: “I like a fresh, glowing look.” Which option should you recommend first?

4. Decision 4: Explain how to build or finish the look. The customer asks: “Can I make it last longer or look more defined?” What is the best response?

Choose an answer, then check the question.

Lesson 5 - The Bronzer

OBC bronzer

Product Overview

An innovative universal bronzer formula that can be used to create a radiant, sun-kissed glow to the skin, with a velvet smooth touch and natural glowing finish.

Its light texture makes it bendable and buildable for a more intense sculpted finish or can be simply applied to bare skin for a natural tanned look.

Contains Hoya Lacunosa Flower Extract which is rich in bioactive compounds and is known for its skin-conditioning properties. Hoya Lacunosa flower extract also helps to soothe, hydrate, and improve skin texture, while also enhancing the skin’s natural barrier and promoting a radiant and smooth complexion overall.

The Bronzer contains spherical powders that allow for a homogenous and smooth application and emollient oils to maintain the physiological hydration of the skin.

What it does

  • Gives the skin a radiant, sun-kissed glow.
  • Adds warmth and definition with a natural, glowing finish.
  • Applies smoothly and evenly for a soft, velvety feel.
  • Helps keep the skin hydrated and comfortable.

How to use it

Light application:

  • Apply to bare skin for a natural, tanned look.

Buildable application:

  • Build it up for a more sculpted, defined finish.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Want a healthy, sun-kissed look.
  • Like a natural glow or a more sculpted finish.
  • Want bronzer that blends easily.
  • Want make-up that still feels good on the skin.

How to sell

  • “This gives your skin a natural, sun-kissed glow.”
  • “You can keep it soft and natural or build it for more definition.”
  • “It blends easily and doesn’t look patchy or heavy.”

Top Tip

Start light and build slowly — this bronzer is designed to be buildable.

When to Recommend

Recommend this when a customer wants warmth, glow, or light contouring without harsh lines.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: “I want a natural glow, not harsh contour”

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
“I want my skin to look warm and healthy, but I don’t want harsh contour lines or anything heavy.”

Your goal: Recommend the right product, explain how it looks on the skin, and guide how to apply it.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the customer’s goal. What is the customer mainly looking for?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best meets this need?

3. Decision 3: Decide how to apply it. The customer adds: “I want it to look very natural.” How should you recommend applying it?

4. Decision 4: Explain how to build the look. The customer asks: “What if I want more definition for the evening?” What is the best response?

Choose an answer, then check the question.
